hero.sub: An open-source training log for cyclists. Self-host it in five minutes, track progression for a lifetime, and never see a paywall.

01.title: Power curves, normalized.
01.body: Mean-maximal power across any date range, season overlays included.

02.title: Fitness, fatigue, form.
02.body: The Banister model on your real training history — not an estimate.

03.title: Bring your history.
03.body: FIT file upload, Strava and Wahoo integrations — all supported.

04.title: Zero telemetry.
04.body: No analytics, no third-party SDKs. Your rides stay on your server.

05.title: Portable by design.
05.body: SQLite under the hood. Export your raw FIT files and profile data as JSON — anytime.

06.title: Know what you trained.
06.body: Every ride is automatically classified — Recovery, Endurance, Tempo, Threshold, VO2 Max, Anaerobic, or Sprint — using Coggan's power model. Override with one click.

07.title: Build structured sessions.
07.body: Design interval workouts with per-step power, HR, or cadence targets — zone numbers, % FTP, or absolute watts. Export to Zwift (.zwo) or as FIT workout files for Wahoo ELEMNT and Garmin bike computers.

selfhost.body: Run openkoutsi anywhere — a Pi in your closet, a €4 VPS, or your home NAS. Docker compose for one-command setup, or build from source. No sign-ups, no pro tier.

createTeam.title: Create a team
createTeam.subtitle: Set up your team account. Your team will be reviewed before you can log in.
createTeam.slugHint: Used in URLs — lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
createTeam.successBody: Your team is pending approval. You will be able to log in once a superadmin approves it.

setup.title: Welcome to openkoutsi
setup.subtitle: Create your first team and administrator account to get started.
setup.slugHint: Used in URLs — lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
setup.success: Team created! You can now log in.
